title: dryopteris pallida subsp. raddeana
intro: Dryopteris pallida subsp. raddeana, also known as a type of fern, is a plant species that belongs to the Dryopteridaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in East Asian cultures, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of Dryopteris pallida subsp. raddeana include reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in traditional East Asian medicine to aid in digestion and relieve menstrual cramps.
